The Collection

Collection of Studio Blown Glass

The Collection of Studio Blown Glass presents outstanding works created by artists of the Pervomaysky Glass Factory during the 1970s and 1980s. Every piece originates from the factory's museum collection, where the finest examples of its artistic glass production were preserved for decades.

The defining characteristic of these works is their exceptional sense of proportion, sculptural elegance, and the remarkable craftsmanship of the master glassblowers. Each vase, vessel, and decorative composition was individually hand-blown at the furnace, making every object a unique work of art.

Although created more than half a century ago, these objects possess a distinctly contemporary aesthetic and can easily be appreciated as modern art pieces. Their refined silhouettes, harmonious proportions, fluid forms, and sophisticated use of color remain strikingly relevant in twenty-first-century interiors. They represent the highest achievements of Soviet studio glass, where technical excellence was combined with the individual artistic vision of each designer.

The collection was created by the leading artists and master glassblowers of the Pervomaysky Glass Factory. Many of these works were exhibited at major national exhibitions, including the Exhibition of Achievements of the National Economy (VDNKh), where they represented the finest accomplishments of Soviet artistic glassmaking. Following these exhibitions, a number of pieces entered museum holdings and prestigious private collections, including those of senior government and Communist Party officials.

Today, the collection stands not only as an important document of the history of Soviet artistic glass but also as a testament to the outstanding quality of its design. Each object possesses independent artistic value and reflects the creative excellence of one of the most accomplished schools of studio blown glass in the second half of the twentieth century.
